_comment_: Master parcel registry. Atlas-grounded facts about cortical regions, independent of any paper or view. View configs reference parcels here by stable ID. When atlas data replaces hand-tuned centroids, only centroid/radius/provenance change. The `note` field is a function-focused, plain-language description used by the glossary tooltip — describes what the region does or why it matters, not where it is.

lang.LanA-IFGorb-L.label: IFG-orbital
lang.LanA-IFGorb-L.note: Orbital portion of Broca's territory. Implicated in higher-level semantic and pragmatic processing — meaning, intent, and discourse-level context in language.

lang.LanB-IFG-L.label: IFG (Broca's)
lang.LanB-IFG-L.note: Classic Broca's area. Associated with speech production and syntactic processing; damage produces non-fluent aphasia — comprehension preserved, but speech becomes laboured and halting.

lang.LanC-MFG-L.label: MFG
lang.LanC-MFG-L.note: Middle frontal portion of the language network. Recruited for verbal working memory during sentence processing — holding words in mind while structure is built.

lang.LanD-AntTemp-L.label: Anterior temporal
lang.LanD-AntTemp-L.note: Semantic hub. Combines word meanings into concepts and stores knowledge about objects, people, and events; degrades selectively in semantic dementia, where word meaning is lost while grammar is preserved.

lang.LanE-PostTemp-L.label: Posterior temporal
lang.LanE-PostTemp-L.note: Wernicke's area. Maps spoken and written word forms to meaning; damage produces fluent but uncomprehended speech — words flow, but content is lost.

tom.TPJ-L.label: TPJ-L
tom.TPJ-L.layCue: left side, where the temporal and parietal lobes meet
tom.TPJ-L.note: Theory-of-mind hub. Engages when reasoning about other people's beliefs, intentions, and knowledge states; left TPJ is recruited especially during sustained social-narrative comprehension.

tom.TPJ-R.label: TPJ-R
tom.TPJ-R.note: Right-hemisphere theory-of-mind hub. Particularly responsive to false-belief reasoning — updating mental models when someone else's perspective differs from your own.

tom.MTG-L.label: MTG-L
tom.MTG-L.note: Middle temporal gyrus, left. Part of the social-narrative network; tracks character mental states and integrates meaning across narrative.

tom.MTG-R.label: MTG-R
tom.MTG-R.note: Right MTG within the bilateral mentalising network. Engaged when modelling others' minds, especially during socially-rich narrative.

yeo.DMN-dmPFC.label: dmPFC
yeo.DMN-dmPFC.note: Dorsomedial prefrontal cortex. Self-referential thought and mentalising — active when you think about your own traits, autobiographical memories, or the inner life of close others.

yeo.DMN-Precuneus.label: Precuneus
yeo.DMN-Precuneus.layCue: middle of the inside surface, toward the back
yeo.DMN-Precuneus.note: Central node of the default mode network. Engaged during mind-wandering, autobiographical memory retrieval, and self-referential thought; one of the most metabolically-active regions during rest.

yeo.DMN-PCC.label: PCC
yeo.DMN-PCC.note: Posterior cingulate cortex. Quiet during external task focus and intensely active during rest, daydreaming, and inward attention — a barometer for whether the brain is turned in or out.

dk.lh-frontal-coarse.label: L frontal (coarse)
dk.lh-frontal-coarse.note: Coarse left-frontal territory used by the original Four Modes view. Covers the broad task-positive frontal cortex — executive control regions in left hemisphere.

dk.lh-parietal-coarse.label: L parietal (coarse)
dk.lh-parietal-coarse.note: Coarse left-parietal territory. Spatial attention and goal-directed control; supports keeping a task in mind while you work on it.

dk.rh-frontal-coarse.label: R frontal (coarse)
dk.rh-frontal-coarse.note: Coarse right-frontal territory. Probabilistic and inductive reasoning — weighing evidence under uncertainty rather than chaining formal rules.

dk.rh-parietal-coarse.label: R parietal (coarse)
dk.rh-parietal-coarse.note: Coarse right-parietal territory. Numerosity and magnitude estimation — comparing quantities, gauging how-likely or how-much rather than computing exact values.

_comment_triple_network_: Triple Network parcels (Phase B). Yeo 2011 7-network atlas regions for CEN; existing yeo.DMN-* + new yeo7.DMN.AG-* angular gyrus parcels for DMN; Seeley 2007 anterior insula + ACC for SN.

yeo7.CEN.dlPFC-L.label: L dlPFC (CEN)
yeo7.CEN.dlPFC-L.layCue: upper front of your forehead, left side
yeo7.CEN.dlPFC-L.note: Left dorsolateral prefrontal cortex. The cognitive-control workhorse: holds task goals in working memory, plans action, and suppresses distraction.

yeo7.CEN.dlPFC-R.label: R dlPFC (CEN)
yeo7.CEN.dlPFC-R.layCue: upper front of your forehead, right side
yeo7.CEN.dlPFC-R.note: Right dorsolateral prefrontal cortex. Mirrors the left in executive control with somewhat greater involvement in inhibition and rule maintenance.

yeo7.CEN.IPL-L.label: L IPL (CEN)
yeo7.CEN.IPL-L.note: Left inferior parietal lobule, control portion. Attentional control and integration of task-relevant signals; works with dlPFC to keep behaviour goal-directed.

yeo7.CEN.IPL-R.label: R IPL (CEN)
yeo7.CEN.IPL-R.note: Right inferior parietal lobule. Sustained attention and goal-directed orientation; important for maintaining vigilance over time.

yeo7.DMN.AG-L.label: L Angular Gyrus (DMN)
yeo7.DMN.AG-L.note: Left angular gyrus, default-mode portion. Semantic integration — combining word meanings, memory, and context to form richer conceptual structures.

yeo7.DMN.AG-R.label: R Angular Gyrus (DMN)
yeo7.DMN.AG-R.note: Right angular gyrus. Bilateral DMN node; supports integrating context and abstract relationships during sustained narrative or rest.

seeley.SN.AI-L.label: L Anterior Insula
seeley.SN.AI-L.layCue: hidden inside the lateral fissure, on the left
seeley.SN.AI-L.note: Left anterior insula. Detects what matters in the moment — salience signals — and gates the switch between executive control (CEN) and default-mode states.

seeley.SN.AI-R.label: R Anterior Insula
seeley.SN.AI-R.layCue: hidden inside the lateral fissure, on the right
seeley.SN.AI-R.note: Right anterior insula. Especially attuned to interoceptive (body-state) signals and emotional salience — what's important in the world right now.

seeley.SN.ACC.label: ACC
seeley.SN.ACC.note: Dorsal anterior cingulate cortex. Conflict monitoring, error detection, and effort allocation; works tightly with the anterior insula in salience-based switching.

_comment_vwfa_: VWFA parcel (Phase C). Cohen 2002 reports the Visual Word Form Area as a focal patch on the left mid-fusiform gyrus, approx MNI (-43, -54, -12). Small radius reflects its reported focal extent (~5mm cluster).

cohen2002.VWFA.label: VWFA
cohen2002.VWFA.layCue: bottom of the brain, left side, where vision meets meaning
cohen2002.VWFA.note: Visual Word Form Area. Activates rapidly and selectively for written words across fonts and presentation; develops only with literacy — readers and non-readers show striking differences in this region.
